{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HR Professional",
  "description": "Mirahub Limited is a service-oriented company based in Lagos, Nigeria, specializing in the establishment and management of mobile device collection and service points. The company supports the repair ecosystem for leading smartphone brands such as TECNO, Infinix, and itel by creating accessible locations where customers can conveniently submit their devices for professional repairs.



With a strategic focus on expansion, Mirahub Limited is actively building new branches, particularly within the Agege axis, to improve service coverage and reduce turnaround time for customers. Their operations emphasize efficiency and quality, supported by structured staff training programs that equip personnel with the skills required for device intake, warranty verification, and excellent customer service. Through its commitment to operational excellence and customer satisfaction, Mirahub Limited plays an important role in strengthening after-sales support within Nigeria&rsquo;s growing smartphone market.



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Professional

Job Location: Ikeja Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time



Job Summary


	The Human Resources (HR) professional is responsible formanagingpeople within theorganization and ensuringa productive, compliant, and positive work environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Identifying hiring needs and creating job descriptions
	Advertising job openings and sourcing candidates
	Screening resumes and conducting interviews
	Coordinating the hiring and onboarding process
	Ensuring employees understand company policies and culture
	Acting as a bridge between management and staff
	Handling employee complaints, conflicts, and grievances
	Promoting a positive and inclusive workplace culture
	Managing resignations, retirements, and terminations
	Ensuring proper offboarding procedures
	Supporting company growth and change management
	Planning workforce strategies
	Building leadership and succession plans
	Amongst others.


Requirements


	He/She must have a Minimum of OND, HND, or B.Sc. in business, people management, and social sciences orany related field.
	3 - 4 years of experience and certificationin Human resource management.(NYSC experience acceptable).


Key Skills Required:


	Communication Skills
	Interpersonal Skills
	Problem-Solving Skills
	Organizational &amp; Time Management Skills.




Salary
N180,000 - N200,000 per month.



Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: mirahubltd@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

HR Professional Job at Mirahub Limited




        
    

        
        Mirahub Limited is a service-oriented company based in Lagos, Nigeria, specializing in the establishment and management of mobile device collection and service points. The company supports the repair ecosystem for leading smartphone brands such as TECNO, Infinix, and itel by creating accessible locations where customers can conveniently submit their devices for professional repairs.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    





With a strategic focus on expansion, Mirahub Limited is actively building new branches, particularly within the Agege axis, to improve service coverage and reduce turnaround time for customers. Their operations emphasize efficiency and quality, supported by structured staff training programs that equip personnel with the skills required for device intake, warranty verification, and excellent customer service. Through its commitment to operational excellence and customer satisfaction, Mirahub Limited plays an important role in strengthening after-sales support within Nigeria’s growing smartphone market.



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Professional

Job Location: Ikeja Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time



Job Summary


	The Human Resources (HR) professional is responsible formanagingpeople within theorganization and ensuringa productive, compliant, and positive work environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Identifying hiring needs and creating job descriptions
	Advertising job openings and sourcing candidates
	Screening resumes and conducting interviews
	Coordinating the hiring and onboarding process
	Ensuring employees understand company policies and culture
	Acting as a bridge between management and staff
	Handling employee complaints, conflicts, and grievances
	Promoting a positive and inclusive workplace culture
	Managing resignations, retirements, and terminations
	Ensuring proper offboarding procedures
	Supporting company growth and change management
	Planning workforce strategies
	Building leadership and succession plans
	Amongst others.


Requirements


	He/She must have a Minimum of OND, HND, or B.Sc. in business, people management, and social sciences orany related field.
	3 - 4 years of experience and certificationin Human resource management.(NYSC experience acceptable).


Key Skills Required:


	Communication Skills
	Interpersonal Skills
	Problem-Solving Skills
	Organizational & Time Management Skills.




Salary
N180,000 - N200,000 per month.



Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Fiber Installation Support Technician",
  "description": "Cobranet Limited; a reputable organization incorporated in 2003 and provides internet solutions. Our footprint and range of products have grown immensely since incorporation and we are proud to provide every segment of society with a reliable link to the digital universe.

Our entire network topology is rugged, dynamic and fully redundant with negligible downtimes: content showing extremely high availability exceeding 99.5%. Our promise is to continue providing innovative solutions at the best feature-to-price and service-to-price ratio.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Fiber Installation Support Technician

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a highly motivated and skilled Fiber Installation Support Technician to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will assist our Fiber teams in executing fiber optic cable installations, ensuring efficient and high-quality work.
	This role involves supporting field operations, performing various tasks, and collaborating with the team to accelerate project timelines and minimize downtime. The ideal candidate must be reachable and available to work on Saturdays and Sundays


Key Responsibilities


	Cable Installation: Assist in running fiber optic cables through ducts, conduits, and other pathways.
	HDPE Duct Laying: Lay and manage High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE) ducts for fiber optic cable protection and organization.
	Interlock Removal: Safely remove interlocks and other obstacles to facilitate smooth cable installation.
	Thrust Boring: Perform thrust boring tasks to create pathways for fiber optic cables under roads, pathways, or other obstacles.
	Field Support: Provide general support to the Fiber team during installations, troubleshooting, and maintenance activities.
	Safety Compliance: Adhere to safety protocols and guidelines to ensure a safe working environment for yourself and others.
	Collaboration: Work effectively with other technicians, engineers, and project managers to ensure projects are completed on time and to specification.
	Documentation: Maintain accurate records of work performed, including cable routes, duct layouts, and any issues encountered.


Requirements


	Education: Diploma or degree in a relevant field (e.g., telecommunications, electrical engineering, or related discipline).
	Experience: Prior experience in fiber optic cable installation, telecommunications, or a similar field is preferred.


Skills:


	Knowledge of fiber optic cables and installation techniques.
	Ability to work in various environments, including outdoor and confined spaces.
	Strong problem-solving and troubleshooting skills.
	Excellent communication and teamwork abilities.
	Physical fitness and ability to lift heavy objects, work at heights, and endure varying weather conditions.


Certifications:


	Relevant certifications in fiber optics or telecommunications are a plus.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment@cobranet.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only qualified candidates will be shortlisted.",

Fiber Installation Support Technician Job at Cobranet Limited




        
    

        
        Cobranet Limited; a reputable organization incorporated in 2003 and provides internet solutions. Our footprint and range of products have grown immensely since incorporation and we are proud to provide every segment of society with a reliable link to the digital universe.

Our entire network topology is rugged, dynamic and fully redundant with negligible downtimes: content showing extremely high availability exceeding 99.5%. Our promise is to continue providing innovative solutions at the best feature-to-price and service-to-price ratio.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Fiber Installation Support Technician

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Summary


	We are seeking a highly motivated and skilled Fiber Installation Support Technician to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will assist our Fiber teams in executing fiber optic cable installations, ensuring efficient and high-quality work.
	This role involves supporting field operations, performing various tasks, and collaborating with the team to accelerate project timelines and minimize downtime. The ideal candidate must be reachable and available to work on Saturdays and Sundays


Key Responsibilities


	Cable Installation: Assist in running fiber optic cables through ducts, conduits, and other pathways.
	HDPE Duct Laying: Lay and manage High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE) ducts for fiber optic cable protection and organization.
	Interlock Removal: Safely remove interlocks and other obstacles to facilitate smooth cable installation.
	Thrust Boring: Perform thrust boring tasks to create pathways for fiber optic cables under roads, pathways, or other obstacles.
	Field Support: Provide general support to the Fiber team during installations, troubleshooting, and maintenance activities.
	Safety Compliance: Adhere to safety protocols and guidelines to ensure a safe working environment for yourself and others.
	Collaboration: Work effectively with other technicians, engineers, and project managers to ensure projects are completed on time and to specification.
	Documentation: Maintain accurate records of work performed, including cable routes, duct layouts, and any issues encountered.


Requirements


	Education: Diploma or degree in a relevant field (e.g., telecommunications, electrical engineering, or related discipline).
	Experience: Prior experience in fiber optic cable installation, telecommunications, or a similar field is preferred.


Skills:


	Knowledge of fiber optic cables and installation techniques.
	Ability to work in various environments, including outdoor and confined spaces.
	Strong problem-solving and troubleshooting skills.
	Excellent communication and teamwork abilities.
	Physical fitness and ability to lift heavy objects, work at heights, and endure varying weather conditions.


Certifications:


	Relevant certifications in fiber optics or telecommunications are a plus.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only qualified candidates will be shortlisted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Receptionist
Job Location: Lagos,
Work Schedule: Monday – Friday

Job Summary


	We are currently seeking a smart, well-presented, and organized female Receptionist to join our team.


Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Stockist",
  "description": "Ascentech Services Limited is a full-fledged Human Resource Solution Provider incorporated in 2013 with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to forging long-term partnerships with our clients by creating and delivering robust and flexible services that address the changing needs of their business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Stockist

Job Location: Agbara, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Green Recycling / Manufacturing

Job Summary


	The Stockist will be responsible for managing inventory movement, monitoring stock levels, and ensuring accurate documentation of materials and products within the company&rsquo;s warehouse/store.
	The ideal candidate should be organized, detail-oriented, and capable of maintaining efficient stock control processes to support smooth operational activities.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND, SSCE, or equivalent qualification.
	1&ndash;2 years of experience in inventory, warehouse, or store management.
	Basic computer knowledge, especially Microsoft Excel and inventory record keeping.
	Good numerical and organizational skills.
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: cv@ascentech.com.ng using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the email.",

Stockist Job at Ascentech Services Limited




        
    

        
        Ascentech Services Limited is a full-fledged Human Resource Solution Provider incorporated in 2013 with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to forging long-term partnerships with our clients by creating and delivering robust and flexible services that address the changing needs of their business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Stockist

Job Location: Agbara, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Green Recycling / Manufacturing

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Stockist will be responsible for managing inventory movement, monitoring stock levels, and ensuring accurate documentation of materials and products within the company’s warehouse/store.
	The ideal candidate should be organized, detail-oriented, and capable of maintaining efficient stock control processes to support smooth operational activities.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND, SSCE, or equivalent qualification.
	1–2 years of experience in inventory, warehouse, or store management.
	Basic computer knowledge, especially Microsoft Excel and inventory record keeping.
	Good numerical and organizational skills.
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Warehouse Supervisor",
  "description": "Renda is a technology solution that aggregates and digitizes access to the end-to-end logistics and fulfillment infrastructure that enables businesses to scale seamlessly and efficiently across Africa.

Renda Limited is a fast-growing technology-driven logistics and fulfillment company headquartered in Lagos, Nigeria. We simplify end-to-end order fulfillment and retail distribution for e-commerce businesses across Africa, connecting them to over 300 warehousing and storage partners and more than 3,000 delivery assets. From storage booking and inventory management to last-mile delivery and cash collection, Renda powers commerce at scale across Nigeria, Kenya, and beyond.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warehouse Supervisor

Job Location: Ilupeju, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Operations &amp; Fulfillment
Reports To: wLaas Manager

Role Overview


	We are seeking a dynamic and results-driven Warehouse Supervisor to oversee daily operations at our fulfillment hub in Lagos.
	Reporting to the wLaas Manager, you will lead a team of warehouse associates to ensure fast, accurate, and safe processing of e-commerce orders on behalf of Renda&#39;s merchant partners.
	This role is central to our promise of reliable, tech-enabled fulfillment delivered on time, every time.


Key Responsibilities
Operations Management:


	Supervise and coordinate all inbound and outbound warehouse activities including receiving, put-away, picking, packing, and dispatch of merchant orders
	Monitor daily order fulfillment targets and SLAs, taking proactive steps to resolve bottlenecks
	Ensure accurate and timely processing of returns, damaged goods, and inventory adjustments
	Oversee the use of Renda&#39;s Warehouse Management System (WMS) for real-time stock tracking and order updates


Team Leadership:


	Lead, schedule, and motivate a team of warehouse operatives across shifts
	Conduct performance reviews, set targets, and provide on-the-job coaching and training
	Foster a culture of accountability, speed, and accuracy aligned with Renda&#39;s operational standards


Inventory &amp; Accuracy:


	Oversee regular cycle counts and full stock audits to maintain inventory integrity
	Investigate and resolve discrepancies between physical stock and system records
	Coordinate with merchant account managers to resolve stock queries and fulfillment issues


Health, Safety &amp; Compliance:


	Enforce all health, safety, and housekeeping standards across the warehouse at all times
	Ensure correct handling, labeling, and storage of all goods, including fragile or high-value items
	Conduct regular safety briefings and ensure all staff comply with Renda&#39;s operational policies


Process Improvement:


	Identify inefficiencies in warehouse workflows and recommend improvements to increase throughput and reduce errors.
	Collaborate with the operations team to implement best practices across Renda&#39;s fulfillment network.
	Generate and review daily/weekly operational reports and share insights with management.


Requirements
Preferred Qualifications:


	OND, HND, or a Degree in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Familiarity with e-commerce platforms and order management systems.
	Experience working in a tech-enabled or startup logistics environment.
	Knowledge of lean warehouse practices or process improvement frameworks.


Other Qualifications:


	3&ndash;5 years of experience in a warehouse, logistics, or e-commerce fulfillment environment.
	1&ndash;2 years in a supervisory or team lead role.
	Strong understanding of fulfillment operations, inventory management, and last-mile logistics.
	Proficiency with Warehouse Management Systems (WMS) and Google Workspace / Microsoft Office.
	Ability to work flexible shifts, including weekends and public holidays.
	Strong communication, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
	Physical fitness to handle the demands of an active warehouse environment.


What We Offer


	Competitive Salary.
	Health insurance.
	A fast-paced, high-growth work environment at the forefront of African logistics.
	Career development opportunities within Rendas expanding operations team.
	A culture that values innovation, ownership, and making things happen.


Method of APplication&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@renda.co using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-30",

Warehouse Supervisor Job at Renda Limited




        
    

        
        Renda is a technology solution that aggregates and digitizes access to the end-to-end logistics and fulfillment infrastructure that enables businesses to scale seamlessly and efficiently across Africa.

Renda Limited is a fast-growing technology-driven logistics and fulfillment company headquartered in Lagos, Nigeria. We simplify end-to-end order fulfillment and retail distribution for e-commerce businesses across Africa, connecting them to over 300 warehousing and storage partners and more than 3,000 delivery assets. From storage booking and inventory management to last-mile delivery and cash collection, Renda powers commerce at scale across Nigeria, Kenya, and beyond.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warehouse Supervisor

Job Location: Ilupeju, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Operations & Fulfillment
Reports To: wLaas Manager

Role Overview


	We are seeking a dynamic and results-driven Warehouse Supervisor to oversee daily operations at our fulfillment hub in Lagos.
	Reporting to the wLaas Manager, you will lead a team of warehouse associates to ensure fast, accurate, and safe processing of e-commerce orders on behalf of Renda's merchant partners.
	This role is central to our promise of reliable, tech-enabled fulfillment delivered on time, every time.


Key Responsibilities
Operations Management:


	Supervise and coordinate all inbound and outbound warehouse activities including receiving, put-away, picking, packing, and dispatch of merchant orders
	Monitor daily order fulfillment targets and SLAs, taking proactive steps to resolve bottlenecks
	Ensure accurate and timely processing of returns, damaged goods, and inventory adjustments
	Oversee the use of Renda's Warehouse Management System (WMS) for real-time stock tracking and order updates


Team Leadership:


	Lead, schedule, and motivate a team of warehouse operatives across shifts
	Conduct performance reviews, set targets, and provide on-the-job coaching and training
	Foster a culture of accountability, speed, and accuracy aligned with Renda's operational standards


Inventory & Accuracy:


	Oversee regular cycle counts and full stock audits to maintain inventory integrity
	Investigate and resolve discrepancies between physical stock and system records
	Coordinate with merchant account managers to resolve stock queries and fulfillment issues


Health, Safety & Compliance:


	Enforce all health, safety, and housekeeping standards across the warehouse at all times
	Ensure correct handling, labeling, and storage of all goods, including fragile or high-value items
	Conduct regular safety briefings and ensure all staff comply with Renda's operational policies


Process Improvement:


	Identify inefficiencies in warehouse workflows and recommend improvements to increase throughput and reduce errors.
	Collaborate with the operations team to implement best practices across Renda's fulfillment network.
	Generate and review daily/weekly operational reports and share insights with management.


Requirements
Preferred Qualifications:


	OND, HND, or a Degree in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Familiarity with e-commerce platforms and order management systems.
	Experience working in a tech-enabled or startup logistics environment.
	Knowledge of lean warehouse practices or process improvement frameworks.


Other Qualifications:


	3–5 years of experience in a warehouse, logistics, or e-commerce fulfillment environment.
	1–2 years in a supervisory or team lead role.
	Strong understanding of fulfillment operations, inventory management, and last-mile logistics.
	Proficiency with Warehouse Management Systems (WMS) and Google Workspace / Microsoft Office.
	Ability to work flexible shifts, including weekends and public holidays.
	Strong communication, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
	Physical fitness to handle the demands of an active warehouse environment.


What We Offer


	Competitive Salary.
	Health insurance.
	A fast-paced, high-growth work environment at the forefront of African logistics.
	Career development opportunities within Rendas expanding operations team.
	A culture that values innovation, ownership, and making things happen.


Method of APplication 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.
